search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk CG32 appetite loss reduced intake weight loss screening
site:nice.org.uk NG12 appetite loss weight loss cancer symptoms
candidate_sources_rejected: suspected-cancer guidance used to infer a malignant cause
automatic supplement, diet, feeding, test, or referral advice
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Appetite loss is nonspecific and recommendation-specific cancer combinations require qualifiers.
Nutrition actions require clinician assessment and decision.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with reduced appetite or intake; children, pregnancy, eating disorders, dysphagia, acute illness, cancer, and end-of-life contexts require tailored evidence.
setting_applicability: Primary-care documentation and follow-up as qualified by the exact source and clinical context.
UAE_applicability: International evidence requires UAE primary-care, laboratory, prescribing, referral, safeguarding, and local-pathway adaptation.
recency_verification: Exact official documents and sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The cited exact source remains current on the issuing organisation website at the review date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact adult nutrition-screening section supports appetite, reduced intake, taste, swallowing, bowel habit, illness, weight loss, BMI context, duration, and impact without assigning malnutrition or cause.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting unintentional weight loss, appetite, taste, swallowing, bowel habit, prolonged illness, BMI, percentage weight loss, reduced intake and future intake risk when actually assessed.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact risk-context section supports clinician-entered measured nutrition status, examination, reviewed results, discussion, and follow-up without calculating risk or generating nutrition support.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-recorded malnutrition or risk criteria and consent or best-interest discussion; no diagnosis, calculation, feeding route, or treatment is generated.

unresolved_source_gaps: Measured intake, weight trajectory, BMI, swallowing safety, mood, pregnancy, cause, diagnosis, tests, dietetic input, nutrition support, referral, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

All previously supported GP legacy-item mappings were conservatively removed because exact item identity, direct support, and item-specific applicability were not independently established under the fail-closed contract.
